summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering budget approval, zoning enforcement, police and fire donations, radio tower construction, and health insurance updates. The board approved the 2022 budget of approximately $12.4 million in revenue and $10.3 million in expenses, authorized up to $90,000 in ARP funds for a radio/broadband tower project at Station 18, and approved an amended health care plan with a reduced 2.5% premium increase. The board also addressed junk vehicle removal, a fund balance adjustment between police and fire departments, and introduced elected officials and staff for the live-stream audience.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for December 1-14, 2021
Accepted $50 donation to the police department
Accepted $25 gift card donation to the fire and EMS department
Approved Resolution R-054-2021 for junk motor vehicle removal at 6952 Goshen Road (first box truck)
Approved Resolution R-055-2021 for junk motor vehicle removal at 6952 Goshen Road (second yellow box truck)
Approved the 2022 budget for Goshen Township as presented
Approved use of no more than $90,000 in ARP funds for radio/broadband tower project at Station 18
Approved contract with Pyramid Network Services Inc. not to exceed $90,000 for electrical work on the tower project
Approved amended 2022 wages for elected officials and non-union employees effective January 2, 2022 (adding firefighter engineer pay rate)
Approved Resolution R-057-2021 authorizing membership in the Coalition of Large Urban Townships (CLOUT)
Approved Resolution R-056-2021 establishing Section 125 premium only plan document and summary plan description for HSA contributions effective January 1, 2022
Approved amended Medical Mutual health care plan premium rates for 2022 with 6% rate reduction
Approved $38,095.80 fund balance adjustment from police district to fire department
Approved request for advance of taxes collected by Clermont County Auditor for 2022
action_items: Steve Pegram to schedule future zoning commission hearings at 6:00 PM on the same nights as trustee meetings to avoid running over
Administration to perform budget true-up in early February 2022 after 2021 year-end reconciliation
Darrell to contact the county about getting a bus turn-around sign on Hill Station Road near Mike Day's area
Laura to process the $38,095.80 fund balance adjustment between fund 2111 (fire) and fund 2081 (police)
key_amounts: $90,000 - maximum ARP funds for radio/broadband tower electrical project at Station 18
$90,000 - contract with Pyramid Network Services for electrical work
$800,000+ - additional ARP stimulus funds expected next year
$38,095.80 - fund balance adjustment from police district to fire department
$25 - gift card donation to fire and EMS department
$400 - anonymous donation plus gift cards to Kroger for community Random Acts of Kindness program
6% total rate reduction on Medical Mutual health care premiums (from 8.5% increase down to 2.5% increase)
Initial insurance renewal quotes started at 46% increase; Anthem lowest was 23%; Medical Mutual came in at 8.5%, then reduced to 2.5%
notable_topics: Radio/broadband communications tower construction behind firehouse (Station 18) to address dead air/dead space issues
ARP (American Rescue Plan) fund usage for tower and fire station emergency power
Fire station emergency generator installation funded by Clermont County
Junk motor vehicle removal at 6952 Goshen Road (two abandoned box trucks)
2022 health insurance plan - significant rate reduction from 8.5% to 2.5% increase
Section 125 cafeteria plan establishment for HSA pre-tax contributions
Shop with a Cop event scheduled for the next day
Fund balance adjustment correction between police and fire departments
Coalition of Large Urban Townships (CLOUT) membership renewal
Amended 2022 wage resolution to include firefighter engineer pay rate
School property sale expected to close December 15
Bus turn-around sign needed on Hill Station Road
Future scheduling of zoning hearings at 6:00 PM before trustee meetings
Introduction of elected officials and staff for live-stream viewers
attendees: Steve Pegram (Administrator/Chief, starting January 2 as Administrator)
Ed Myers (Fire Chief, not present - excused)
School representative (mentioned Superintendent Edwards and school property sale)
notes: The meeting was preceded by a zoning commission hearing at 6:30 PM that ran over into the 7 PM trustee meeting. Steve Pegram will transition to Township Administrator role starting January 2, 2022, and Ed Myers will become Fire Chief on the same date. Goshen Township is in the top 5% of Ohio's approximately 1,800 townships by size, qualifying as a 'large urban township.' The $38,095.80 fund balance error had been buried in the general ledger and was not caught during prior audits. A school property sale was expected to close December 15 after multiple attempts with different buyers. An anonymous donation of $400 and Kroger gift cards was made in memory of someone named Jacob for the Random Acts of Kindness program. The tornado damage in western Kentucky was referenced during the invocation, and Trustee Hausman mentioned his family narrowly avoided the tornado.
title: BOT Mtg 12.14.2021.mp4
